title: Nickel Market Recalibrates After Explosive Trading Week
summary: Nickel prices stabilized after a volatile week driven by Indonesian policy signals and increased Chinese buying, with inventory levels and regulatory factors influencing the market swing. title: Nickel Prices Ready to Rise as Supply Tightens in Fourth Quarter
summary: The global nickel market is reaching a critical inflection point as supply and demand dynamics converge, potentially ending the prolonged range-bound pricing between $15,000-$15,800 per ton that persisted since April 2025. Indonesia's strategic shift represents the most significant development, with Western Mines Group discovering a new large ultramafic deposit in Western Australia.

title: Nickel's New Era: Rising Prices, Growing EV Demand
summary: Indonesia's coordinated supply management strategy has driven nickel prices from $14,200 to $16,500 per ton since mid-December 2025, with further advances toward $18,500-$20,000 expected. As Indonesia controls approximately two-thirds of global supply, it has implemented measures including reducing mining license terms, closing mines for forestry violations, and banning new HPAL and NPI processing plants.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: The world's biggest nickel mine in Indonesia has been told to drastically cut output as the country seeks to support global prices. title: Unleashing sustainable nickel production for a net zero future

title: Innovations in Sustainable Nickel Production: The Path to Low-Cost Solutions
